scenario: A local OpenClaw gateway user can load the plugin and call loo_* tools to search, describe, expand, dry-run, and reason about sessions from bounded summaries without raw transcript access.
expected_public_safe_evidence: describe and direct-session/query expand profile names
gateway setupStatus, setupBlockers, and setupGuidance for first-run credential blockers
agent reasoning note based only on safe summaries
pass_criteria: The npm package includes an agent-facing skill/playbook for the canonical staged recall and dry-run workflows.
OpenClaw gateway can discover the plugin or the issue records why the gateway is unavailable.
Agent can search sessions through loo_* read tools and receive source-prefixed refs.
Agent can describe and expand selected refs within a bounded token profile.
Agent can dry-run a control action and reason about the audit id without sending a live action.
Evidence contains public-safe summaries, counts, refs, hashes, setupStatus classifications, and blocker codes only.
fail_criteria: Agent must read raw transcripts to answer the session-management question.
Gateway exposes live mutation without dry-run approval proof.
Evidence captures local DBs, transcript files, screenshots, prompt text, or secrets.
Tool output is too vague for the agent to choose a safe next action.
known_gaps: The first-class agent usage skill is packaged, but the full agent workflow still needs the issue #234 gateway dogfood scenario.
This scorecard requires a local OpenClaw gateway installation or a documented unavailable-gateway blocker.
Fresh OpenClaw profiles may need scoped gateway credentials or profile/device pairing before tool-smoke can call tools; `setupStatus.classification` must distinguish this from package failure.
Full usability proof needs a bounded real-session smoke after indexing is current.
next_action: Run the issue #234 read-only gateway dogfood scenario using only loo_* tools and record public-safe summaries.
proof_boundary: This issue proves the packaged agent skill/playbook and docs links only; full local-agent usability remains pending until the gateway scenario is run.
